documents.title: Dartmouth College public AI posture sources

ai_strategy.summary: Dartmouth is expanding AI coursework, grants, a faculty leadership group, Thayer policy guidance, and an institutional AWS/Anthropic rollout while emphasizing human judgment and ethics.

key_provisions: Use institutionally reviewed AI tools for sensitive university work.
Protect restricted, confidential, student, personnel, and research data when using AI tools.
Verify AI output and keep humans accountable for academic, administrative, and research decisions.
ai_policy.summary: Dartmouth College's public AI policy posture is cautious and emphasizes responsible use, privacy, and human accountability based on reviewed sources.

bodies.name: Dartmouth College AI governance and support functions
bodies.scope: Dartmouth is expanding AI coursework, grants, a faculty leadership group, Thayer policy guidance, and an institutional AWS/Anthropic rollout while emphasizing human judgment and ethics.

governance_structure.summary: Governance appears distributed across AI-facing offices, centers, technology units, teaching units, or committees; no standalone chief AI officer was confirmed unless named in leadership.

initiatives.description: Dartmouth College has public AI guidance, governance, or strategy resources that frame responsible adoption, privacy, academic integrity, and human oversight.
initiatives.significance: This is a concrete R1 signal of AI moving from isolated experimentation toward governed, mission-linked adoption.

initiatives.title: Lifetime career center with AI-enabled tools and outcome metrics
initiatives.description: Dartmouth's Center for Professional Development is being rebuilt as a lifetime career center with AI-enabled tools that students will be able to access via a Dartmouth login. The public announcement discloses a $94 million commitment and reports recent outcomes including increased coaching demand, a 337% rise in employer recruiting events, 82% internship and job application success among students who used center resources, and a 96.1% six-month career outcome rate for the Class of 2024.
initiatives.significance: The initiative provides unusually explicit public usage and outcome signals for an AI-adjacent student-success service.

investment.funding_source: Dartmouth institutional and philanthropic career-center commitment
investment.description: Dartmouth publicly describes a $94 million commitment for the lifetime career center; the announcement includes AI tools but does not itemize the AI portion separately.

collaborations.description: The career center expansion includes employer recruiting growth and AI-enabled student career tooling.

regulatory_context.summary: State/system context was checked for NH. Relevant public AI or system governance links are included where found.

narrative_summary: Dartmouth College is an R1 institution whose public AI posture is best understood as follows: Dartmouth is expanding AI coursework, grants, a faculty leadership group, Thayer policy guidance, and an institutional AWS/Anthropic rollout while emphasizing human judgment and ethics. The evidence touches governance and policy, teaching and learning, research and innovation, enterprise enablement, student experience, workforce development, and equity or public-impact dimensions. The strongest public sources are first-party AI hubs, guidance pages, research center pages, academic-program materials, and state or system policy resources. Compared with the most centralized AI adopters, several details remain opaque, including named executive AI ownership, exact enterprise user counts, and total AI spending. Even so, the reviewed public record supports a medium-confidence assessment that AI is moving into curriculum, research infrastructure, technology governance, and student or workforce-facing programs rather than remaining purely ad hoc.

gaps_acknowledged: No public chief AI officer was confirmed in reviewed sources.
The lifetime career center's $94 million commitment and outcomes are public, but the AI-tool portion is not itemized separately.
Discovery Cluster GPU types are public, but Dartmouth does not publish a total GPU count in the reviewed source.
Exact enterprise AI user counts or contract values were not public for the AWS/Anthropic institutional rollout.

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: balanced enterprise.
